jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager
jobs.jd_full: DataRobot está buscando um Senior Technical Program Manager para trabalhar de perto com as equipes de Produto, Engenharia, GTM e Suporte, trazendo estrutura e velocidade ao desenvolvimento de produtos de IA complexos. O candidato ideal terá experiência em gerenciamento de programas técnicos, habilidades de liderança e será capaz de melhorar como planejamos, executamos e entregamos iniciativas interfuncionais. Responsabilidades incluem projetar e evoluir programas interfuncionais que permitam o envio de capacidades preditivas e generativas com rapidez e rigor, além de melhorar como planejamos, executamos e entregamos iniciativas interfuncionais. É necessário estar preparado para experimentar e iterar em programas com uma pequena equipe de TPM e impulsionar a execução de P&D em programas de alto impacto.

jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager - AI/ML Hardware Health & Stability, Global Data Center Operations
jobs.jd_full: A equipe de Operações Centrais da AWS está buscando um Senior Technical Program Manager para impulsionar a saúde, estabilidade e excelência operacional de novos deployments de hardware em nosso centro de dados global. Este papel combina gerenciamento de programas técnicos com gerenciamento estratégico de contas para garantir que nossa infraestrutura de GenAI e computação de alto desempenho entregue o máximo valor aos clientes. O candidato ideal terá experiência em gerenciamento de programas técnicos, habilidades de liderança e será capaz de trabalhar na interseção de engenharia de hardware, operações de centro de dados e equipes de serviço como EC2, traduzindo dados técnicos complexos em insights acionáveis e liderando programas que aceleram a entrega de capacidade enquanto mantêm os mais altos padrões de saúde operacional.

jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager, Ring AI
jobs.jd_full: A Ring está buscando um Senior Technical Program Manager para liderar o desenvolvimento de soluções de software altamente escaláveis que alimentam câmeras Ring, campainhas de vídeo e outros dispositivos de segurança. Neste papel, você trabalhará de perto com equipes de software, ciência de IA, hardware e produto para entregar experiências inovadoras aos clientes em todo o mundo. O candidato ideal terá um histórico técnico, será detalhista, possuirá fortes habilidades de comunicação, será proficiente em orquestrar desenvolvimento em múltiplos sites e terá excelentes habilidades de resolução de problemas. Você formará equipes de programa, dividirá iniciativas complexas em fluxos de trabalho bem definidos, desenvolverá planos de programa robustos e impulsionará cronogramas para garantir a conclusão oportuna dos entregáveis.

jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager (TPM), Amazon Catalog - Product Relationships
jobs.jd_full: A equipe de Catálogo da Amazon está buscando um Senior Technical Program Manager para impulsionar programas complexos e interfuncionais que permitem novos tipos de relacionamentos de produtos em nosso Catálogo. Neste papel, você definirá os sistemas e políticas para como as marcas comercializam seus produtos na Amazon, garantindo a melhor experiência de compra possível para os clientes. Você permitirá que a Amazon ofereça maneiras inovadoras para os clientes encontrarem produtos relacionados, incluindo versões mais recentes, coleções de marcas e linhas de produtos. Trabalhando em escala do Catálogo da Amazon (bilhões de produtos e relacionamentos), você fará parceria com equipes de engenharia e produto para construir novas APIs, definir modelos de dados, desenvolver métricas de qualidade e impulsionar iniciativas de aquisição e melhoria de dados aproveitando IA generativa.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Tech
jobs.jd_full: A Amazon está buscando um Senior Product Manager - Tech para influenciar múltiplas organizações da empresa, ser um comunicador excepcional e pensador analítico, com histórico de entrega de resultados. O candidato ideal terá resolvido problemas complicados com soluções elegantes e simples, será capaz de prosperar e ter sucesso em um ambiente empreendedor, não sendo impedido por ambiguidade ou prioridades concorrentes. O candidato ideal terá habilidades em estratégia e direção de produto, planejamento de roadmap, definição de requisitos e trabalhará com equipes técnicas para elaborar documentos de requisitos de negócios, influenciar o design e a arquitetura de soluções técnicas.

jobs.title: Operations software assistant
jobs.jd_full:
Operations software assistant

 

Bulldog is a UK agency helping marketing and SEO teams increase their visibility on Google and AI through link building and digital PR. We're a team of 12, with expertise in SEO, content, link building, and PR, based in the UK, Serbia, and North Macedonia.

 

We are looking for a process-driven and intuitive person to work alongside our Operations Manager in overhauling our internal processes into a modern, streamlined way of working. We've already started implementing this as a proof of concept, with incredible results. We now need an extra pair of creative hands to move faster and help execute improvements.

 

The role

 

  • Help review processes and improvements in ways of working.

  • Help build additional internal tools to improve delivery and efficiency.

  • Use Claude AI to code, with clear operational thinking on what to build and why.

  • Own tool maintenance, including fixes, improvements and updates.

  • Monitor internal tools and portals to make sure they run smoothly.

  • Keep the team updated on tool changes, updates and how things work.

  • Work with the Operations Manager to find ways to save the company time on tasks.

  • Take on a wide range of agency-improving tasks with the Operations Manager.

 

Skills & experience

 

  • Nice to have an understanding of link building, SEO and the AI ranking space.

  • Intuitive thinking on how to improve processes so they can scale.

  • Strong English and clear communication on project progress.

  • Comfortable using Claude AI to code and create tools.

  • Enjoys improving processes and is confident in independently owning projects.

 

Working for Bulldog

 

  • A friendly, passionate team that takes quality seriously.

  • Join a talented team who work closely together every day.

  • A flexible and remote-first work culture, enjoy your birthday off, fully paid.

You'll be hired as a freelancer on 35 hours per week to start, with room to grow. You'll have a contract in place with notice periods, so you have security with us from the start.

jobs.title: Senior Engineering Manager
jobs.jd_full:
POS-32107

Senior Engineering Manager, CRM Product Platform

Role Summary

Our mission at HubSpot is to help millions of organizations grow better. As a Senior Engineering Manager in the Product Platform Product Line, you’ll lead high-impact product areas that power how customers experience and scale with HubSpot’s CRM. You’ll guide multiple engineering teams building core surfaces used by more than a million users, while shaping the future of AI-powered product experiences. This role blends deep technical leadership with strong product thinking - driving both innovation and operational excellence.

What You’ll Do

  • Lead and grow several engineering teams in HubSpot’s CRM Data Visualisation group, which powers the CRM Index Page and its table, board, gantt, calendar, and map views. You’ll deliver high-quality, scalable product experiences across HubSpot’s highest-traffic product surfaces.
  • Partner closely with Product, Design, and cross-functional leaders to define and execute roadmap priorities.
  • Drive the development and launch of AI Agents, AI-powered features, and AI driven insights that enhance customer value.
  • Establish and uphold a high-performance engineering culture, including strong technical standards and accountability, performance management, and career development.
  • Contribute hands-on technical leadership, guiding architecture and participating in critical technical discussions across the stack.
  • Align teams to support broader CRM platform infrastructure and cross-hub initiatives.
  • Navigate ambiguity and drive clarity, enabling teams to move quickly, experiment, and iterate effectively.

Required Qualifications

  • Proven experience leading and scaling engineering teams (15+ engineers), including team leads and senior technical individual contributors.
  • Strong product engineering background, with experience building and scaling SaaS products from early stages through high growth. You have experience shaping feature delivery from early stage validation, to late stage maturity.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead full-stack product discussions and influence both frontend and backend architecture.
  • Hands-on technical credibility with a track record of contributing to engineering decisions and mentoring teams.
  • Experience driving high performance through goal setting, feedback, and performance management.
  • Ability to operate effectively in ambiguous environments and make informed decisions without complete data.
  • Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, with experience partnering across product, design, and engineering.
  • Experience leveraging or integrating AI/GenAI into product development workflows or features.

Nice-to-Have Qualifications

  • Experience working on high-scale customer-facing platforms or CRM systems.
  • Background in both upmarket and downmarket product experiences.
  • Experience building dynamic UI systems, modern frontend architectures, or production quality agentic experiences.

Where You’ll Work

  • Location: United States
  • Work location preference eligibility: Flexible (Remote, Hybrid, or Onsite)
  • Travel/Shift: Some travel required for cross-functional collaboration and team alignment

Pay & Benefits

The cash compensation below includes base salary, on-target commission for employees in eligible roles, and annual bonus targets under HubSpot’s bonus plan for eligible roles. In addition to cash compensation, some roles are eligible to participate in HubSpot’s equity plan to receive restricted stock units (RSUs). Some roles may also be eligible for overtime pay. Individual compensation packages are tailored to your skills, experience, qualifications, and other job-related reasons.

This resource will help guide how we recommend thinking about the range you see. Learn more about HubSpot’s compensation philosophy.

Benefits are also an important piece of your total compensation package. Explore the benefits and perks HubSpot offers to help employees grow better.

At HubSpot, fair compensation practices aren’t just about checking off the box for legal compliance. It’s about living out our value of transparency with our employees, candidates, and community.

Annual Cash Compensation Range:$259,128—$414,652 USD

We know the confidence gap and impostor syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates, so please don’t hesitate to apply — we’d love to hear from you.

If you need accommodations or assistance due to a disability, please reach out to us using this form.

At HubSpot, we value both flexibility and connection. Whether you’re a Remote employee or work from the Office, we want you to start your journey here by building strong connections with your team and peers. If you are joining our Engineering team, you will be required to attend a regional HubSpot office for in-person onboarding. If you join our broader Product team, you’ll also attend other in-person events, such as your Product Group Summit and other gatherings, to continue building on those connections.

If you require an accommodation due to travel limitations or other reasons, please inform your recruiter during the hiring process. We are committed to supporting candidates who may need alternative arrangements

Massachusetts Applicants: It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Germany Applicants: (m/f/d) - link to HubSpot's Career Diversity page here.

India Applicants: link to HubSpot India's equal opportunity policy here.

About HubSpot

HubSpot (NYSE: HUBS) is an AI-powered customer platform with all the software, integrations, and resources customers need to connect marketing, sales, and service. HubSpot's connected platform enables businesses to grow faster by focusing on what matters most: customers. 

At HubSpot, bold is our baseline. Our employees around the globe move fast, stay customer-obsessed, and win together. Our culture is grounded in four commitments: Solve for the Customer, Be Bold, Learn Fast, Align, Adapt & Go!, and Deliver with HEART. These commitments shape how we work, lead, and grow.

We’re building a company where people can do their best work. We focus on brilliant work, not badge swipes. By combining clarity, ownership, and trust, we create space for big thinking and meaningful progress. And we know that when our employees grow, our customers do too.

Recognized globally for our award-winning culture by Comparably, Glassdoor, Fortune, and more, HubSpot is headquartered in Cambridge, MA, with employees and offices around the world.

Explore more:

  • HubSpot Careers
  • Life at HubSpot on Instagram

HubSpot may use AI to help screen or assess candidates, but all hiring decisions are always human. More information can be found here. By submitting your application, you agree that HubSpot may collect your personal data for recruiting, global organization planning, and related purposes. We may use CLEAR ID Verification during the hiring process to confirm your identity and help maintain a safe, secure, and trusted experience for all candidates. Refer to HubSpot's Recruiting Privacy Notice for details on data processing and your rights.

jobs.title: Sr Product Manager, Oncology Japan
jobs.jd_full:
Location: United States (Willingness to travel to Japan 1-2x a year - Japanese fluency in language and culture is a requirement)
Company Overview: Natera, Inc. is a clinical genetic testing company based in Austin, Texas that specializes in non-invasive, cell-free DNA (cfDNA) testing technology, with a focus on women’s health, cancer, and organ health. Natera’s proprietary technology combines novel molecular biology techniques with a suite of bioinformatics software that allows detection down to a single molecule in a tube of blood. Natera operates CAP-accredited laboratories certified under the Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) in San Carlos, California, and Austin, Texas.
Position Summary: The mission of the Oncology User Experience Product team is to ensure that our customers and patients receive best-in-class experiences that resolve frictions to better patient care. The Senior Product Manager for Oncology Provider Solutions, Japan, will own the scale of our key product lines in the Japanese market and will join a growing team that shares the critical role of being the voice of our many internal and external users. Additionally, you’ll have the chance to make meaningful contributions to the broader Oncology portfolio, supporting initiatives for products in the primary US business as well.
You will work closely with business development, regulatory, engineering, design, and operations to create a vision, strategy, and thorough plan to scale our Provider Portal and drive growth in our clinical workflows. You will be acting as a full-stack PM, leading a cross-functional team in conducting user interviews, uncovering user pain points, gathering requirements, writing BRD/PRDs, creating user stories, defining acceptance criteria, and leading the scale of our Provider Portal for Japan. You will play a central role in developing country-wide user experiences that will reach millions of patients and their families.
About You: You care about improving outcomes for patients and clinicians and want your work to have real impact. You're comfortable navigating ambiguity, working across teams, and making decisions with imperfect information. You give and receive candid feedback, take ownership of hard problems, and don't wait to be told what to do. You possess Japanese fluency in language, culture, and regulation, and are excited by the opportunity to grow our business in Japan where there is enormous market potential and help drive a paradigm shift in how oncology care is delivered.
If you enjoy moving quickly, accomplishing aspirational goals in an ever-changing environment, and launching truly impactful products, this role is for you.
Primary Responsibilities

  • Develop a deep understanding and appreciation of our users, including customers, patients, business partners, the commercial team, and customer operations, as well as their workflows, behaviors, and environments
  • Partner with various stakeholders such as commercial product team, regulatory affairs, customer operations, and the local Japanese business team to gather insights and align on priorities
  • Collaborate with our full suite of UX product development teams to translate business and market needs into clear, well-scoped requirements for the Japanese market
  • Maintain a vision of industry trends and competitive landscape to ensure you know what makes a best-in-class user experience for your product line
  • Develop well-thought, user-centric strategies and roadmap for your products
  • Own product development lifecycle from design to deployment
  • Define what success looks like for your product, measure it, and drive improvements
  • Communicate with senior-level stakeholders to champion your roadmap and gain buy-in
  • Help define and enhance the culture of the Oncology UXPM team, our Product org, and Natera at large

Qualifications

  • Japanese fluency in language and culture
  • Understands and able to exhibit Japanese business acumen
  • Empathetic, user-centric thinker with exceptional integrity
  • 3-5+ years of experience in software product management, ideally in the digital health, healthcare, or biotech industry
  • Experience with regulated products and regulatory affairs in the US and/or abroad
  • Extensive experience in conceptualizing, defining, and creating high-quality customer experiences
  • Experience building solutions from start to finish
  • Excellent communication, facilitation, presentation, relationship-building, and analytical skills
  • A successful track record of cross-functional leadership
  • Well-developed systems thinking and problem-solving frameworks
  • Desire to roll sleeves up and get projects across the finish line
  • Willingness to travel to Japan 1-2x per calendar year

The pay range is listed and actual compensation packages are based on a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to skill set, years & depth of experience, certifications and specific office location. This may differ in other locations due to cost of labor considerations.Remote USA$120,100—$150,100 USD

OUR OPPORTUNITY

Natera™ is a global leader in cell-free DNA (cfDNA) testing, dedicated to oncology, women’s health, and organ health. Our aim is to make personalized genetic testing and diagnostics part of the standard of care to protect health and enable earlier and more targeted interventions that lead to longer, healthier lives.

The Natera team consists of highly dedicated statisticians, geneticists, doctors, laboratory scientists, business professionals, software engineers and many other professionals from world-class institutions, who care deeply for our work and each other. When you join Natera, you’ll work hard and grow quickly. Working alongside the elite of the industry, you’ll be stretched and challenged, and take pride in being part of a company that is changing the landscape of genetic disease management.

WHAT WE OFFER

Competitive Benefits - Employee benefits include comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life and disability plans for eligible employees and their dependents. Additionally, Natera employees and their immediate families receive free testing in addition to fertility care benefits. Other benefits include pregnancy and baby bonding leave, 401k benefits, commuter benefits and much more. We also offer a generous employee referral program!

For more information, visit www.natera.com.

Natera is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to ensuring a diverse and inclusive workplace environment, and welcome people of different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives. Inclusive collaboration benefits our employees, our community and our patients, and is critical to our mission of changing the management of disease worldwide.

All qualified applicants are encouraged to apply, and will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with applicable laws.

If you are based in California, we encourage you to read this important information for California residents. 

Link: https://www.natera.com/notice-of-data-collection-california-residents/

Please be advised that Natera will reach out to candidates with a @natera.com email domain ONLY. Email communications from all other domain names are not from Natera or its employees and are fraudulent. Natera does not request interviews via text messages and does not ask for personal information until a candidate has engaged with the company and has spoken to a recruiter and the hiring team. Natera takes cyber crimes seriously, and will collaborate with law enforcement authorities to prosecute any related cyber crimes.

jobs.title: Gestionnaire de produit - Acquisition & finance intégrée / Product Manager - Acq
jobs.jd_full:
Gestionnaire de produit – Acquisition & finance intégrée

Nous sommes à la recherche d’un gestionnaire de produit pour diriger et faire évoluer nos capacités d’acquisition et de finance intégrée, en prenant en charge les transactions avec carte présente (card-present) et sans carte (card-not-present) au sein de notre base de marchands.

Ce rôle se situe à l’intersection du traitement des paiements, de l’intégration des marchands (onboarding), de l’orchestration des plateformes et de la conformité réglementaire. Il est essentiel à la livraison de solutions d’acquisition sécurisées, fiables et concurrentielles pour les commerçants canadiens.

Vous serez responsable de bout en bout du cycle de vie du produit d’acquisition — de la stratégie et de la feuille de route jusqu’à la livraison, l’optimisation et la mise à l’échelle — avec un accent sur les plateformes et flux de travail qui soutiennent l’orchestration de l’intégration des marchands, l’autorisation, la compensation (clearing), le règlement (settlement), la finance intégrée et les rapports.

Vous collaborerez étroitement avec les équipes Technologie, Opérations, Risque, Conformité, Ventes, ainsi qu’avec des partenaires externes tels que les processeurs de paiement, les réseaux de paiement et les institutions financières.

Il s’agit d’un rôle de contributeur individuel (« individual contributor ») avec un fort leadership transversal et une grande visibilité organisationnelle.

Gestion de produit – Acquisition

  • Définir et piloter la stratégie produit et la feuille de route des capacités d’acquisition, incluant l’autorisation, la compensation, le règlement, la gestion des rétrofacturations (chargebacks) et les rapports, pour les flux card-present et card-not-present.
  • Diriger la définition, la priorisation et la livraison des produits d’orchestration de l’intégration des marchands — incluant les workflows de demande marchand, les points d’intégration avec la souscription (underwriting), les API d’activation (boarding) et les processus de mise en service.
  • Définir et gérer les exigences liées à l’intégration des marchands, en assurant des expériences fluides, évolutives et conformes pour les canaux directs, ISO, ISV et PayFac.
  • Identifier les opportunités de réduire les frictions à l’intégration, d’accélérer le délai de mise en service (time-to-live) et d’augmenter l’automatisation des processus.
  • Collaborer avec les équipes Risque, Conformité et Opérations afin d’intégrer les contrôles AML (lutte contre le blanchiment d’argent), KYC (connaissance du client), prévention de la fraude et gestion des litiges dans les flux d’intégration et d’acquisition.
  • Travailler avec les équipes technologiques pour définir les API, les standards d’intégration et les modèles de données soutenant l’intégration des marchands et les opérations d’acquisition à grande échelle.
  • S’assurer que les plateformes d’acquisition et d’intégration respectent les règles des réseaux de paiement, les exigences réglementaires et les contraintes opérationnelles dans des contextes canadiens et transfrontaliers.
  • Piloter l’analyse des incidents et les initiatives d’amélioration continue liées à la performance des plateformes, aux échecs d’intégration et aux exceptions de règlement.

Finance intégrée (Embedded Finance)

  • Définir et piloter la stratégie et la livraison des capacités de finance intégrée, incluant le financement, le fonds de roulement et les services financiers à valeur ajoutée intégrés à l’expérience d’acquisition.
  • Collaborer avec les équipes Ventes et la direction afin d’enrichir l’offre de solutions de finance intégrée, définir les stratégies de mise en marché (go-to-market) et soutenir les décisions de structuration commerciale.
  • Définir les exigences produit pour les intégrations de finance intégrée, en assurant leur alignement avec l’infrastructure d’acquisition, les obligations de conformité et les modèles de livraison des partenaires.
  • Identifier et évaluer de nouvelles opportunités en finance intégrée afin d’accroître l’engagement des marchands et diversifier les sources de revenus.

Qualifications et experience

  • 5+ années d’expérience en gestion de produit dans les paiements, la fintech ou les services financiers.
  • Solide compréhension des fondamentaux de l’acquisition : autorisation, compensation, règlement, interchange et gestion des rétrofacturations.
  • Expérience concrète dans la définition des exigences pour des plateformes d’intégration des marchands, des workflows d’orchestration ou des systèmes d’opérations d’acquisition.
  • Connaissance des réseaux et standards de paiement canadiens (Interac, EMV, PCI DSS).
  • Expérience avec les modèles opérationnels PayFac, ISO ou ISV et compréhension des différences dans les processus d’intégration des marchands selon ces canaux.
  • Capacité démontrée à piloter des initiatives complexes et transversales dans des environnements réglementés.
  • Excellentes compétences en communication et en documentation, tant avec des parties prenantes techniques que non techniques.
  • Capacité à prendre des décisions basées sur les données et à effectuer des arbitrages commerciaux éclairés.

Atouts supplémentaires

  • Expérience avec des produits de finance intégrée tels que les avances de fonds marchands (merchant cash advance), le financement du fonds de roulement ou les solutions de prêt intégrées.
  • Connaissance des principes UX/UI et expérience de collaboration avec des équipes de design pour concevoir des parcours marchands et des interfaces.
  • Expérience en gestion des risques, fraude ou conformité dans des environnements d’acquisition ou d’intégration marchands.
  • Exposition à des environnements d’acquisition omnicanal ou à des écosystèmes POS intégrés.
  • Expérience dans les secteurs de l’hôtellerie ou du commerce de détail.
  • Bilinguisme (français/anglais).

À propos de nous

  • Nous offrons des solutions intégrées de paiement et de point de vente aux restaurants, hôtels et bars.
  • Nous sommes reconnus pour nos systèmes POS de pointe (Veloce et Maitre’D).
  • Nous opérons au Canada, aux États-Unis et en Europe.

Ce que nous offrons

  • Santé et bien-être : Assurance médicale et dentaire dès le jour 1, ainsi que télémédecine 24/7.
  • Sécurité financière : Programme de cotisation REER avec participation de l’employeur.
  • Équilibre travail-vie personnelle : Vacances généreuses et journées personnelles.
  • Croissance et impact : Environnement en forte croissance où votre contribution a un impact concret.
  • Culture : Équipe collaborative et inclusive valorisant l’innovation et le développement.

Product Manager – Acquiring & Embedded Finance

We are seeking a Product Manager to lead and evolve our acquiring and embedded finance capabilities, supporting both card-present and card-not-present transactions across our merchant base.

This role sits at the intersection of payment processing, merchant onboarding, platform orchestration, and regulatory compliance. It is critical to delivering secure, reliable, and competitive acquiring solutions for Canadian merchants.

You will have end-to-end ownership of the acquiring product lifecycle — from strategy and roadmap development through platform delivery, optimization, and scaling — with a focus on the platforms and workflows that power merchant onboarding orchestration, authorization, clearing, settlement, embedded finance, and reporting.

You will work closely with Technology, Operations, Risk, Compliance, Sales, and external partners including payment processors, payment networks, and financial institution partners.

This is a hands-on individual contributor role with significant cross-functional leadership and organizational visibility.

Acquiring Product Management

  • Own the product strategy and roadmap for acquiring capabilities, including authorization, clearing, settlement, chargebacks, and reporting across card-present and card-not-present transaction flows.
  • Lead the definition, prioritization, and delivery of merchant platform onboarding orchestration products — including merchant application workflows, underwriting integration touchpoints, boarding APIs, and activation processes.
  • Define and manage requirements for merchant onboarding orchestration, ensuring seamless, scalable, and compliant boarding experiences across direct, ISO, ISV, and PayFac channels.
  • Identify opportunities to reduce onboarding friction, improve time-to-live for new merchants, and increase boarding automation across partner and direct segments.
  • Partner with Risk, Compliance, and Operations to embed AML, KYC, fraud prevention, and dispute management controls into onboarding and acquiring workflows.
  • Collaborate with Technology to define platform APIs, integration standards, and data models that support scalable merchant onboarding and acquiring operations.
  • Ensure acquiring and onboarding platforms remain aligned with network rules, regulatory requirements, and operational constraints across Canadian and cross-border contexts.
  • Lead incident analysis and continuous improvement initiatives related to acquiring platform performance, onboarding failures, and settlement exceptions.

Embedded Finance

  • Drive the strategy and delivery of embedded finance capabilities, including financing, working capital, and value-added financial services integrated into the merchant acquiring experience.
  • Partner with Sales and leadership to enhance embedded finance solution offerings, define go-to-market models, and support commercial packaging decisions.
  • Define product requirements for embedded finance integrations, ensuring alignment with acquiring infrastructure, compliance obligations, and partner delivery models.
  • Identify and evaluate new embedded finance opportunities that deepen merchant engagement and expand revenue streams.

Qualifications & Experience

  • 5+ years of product management experience in payments, fintech, or financial services.
  • Strong understanding of acquiring fundamentals including authorization, clearing, settlement, interchange, and chargeback management.
  • Hands-on experience defining requirements for merchant onboarding platforms, boarding orchestration workflows, or acquiring operations systems.
  • Familiarity with Canadian payment networks and standards (Interac, EMV, PCI DSS).
  • Experience working across PayFac, ISO, or ISV operating models and understanding of how merchant boarding differs across these channels.
  • Proven ability to lead complex, cross-functional initiatives in regulated environments.
  • Strong communication and documentation skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Comfortable making data-driven decisions and commercially informed trade-offs.

Additional Assets

  • Experience with embedded finance products such as merchant cash advance, working capital, or integrated lending solutions.
  • Familiarity with UX/UI principles and experience collaborating with design teams to shape merchant-facing workflows, onboarding experiences, or platform interfaces.
  • Background in risk, fraud, or compliance within acquiring or merchant onboarding environments.
  • Exposure to omnichannel acquiring or integrated POS ecosystems from a platform perspective.
  • Experience supporting hospitality or retail merchant segments.
  • Bilingual (English/French).

About Us

  • We provide fully integrated payment and POS solutions to restaurants, hotels, and bars.
  • We are recognized for our industry-leading POS systems (Veloce and Maitre’D).
  • We operate across Canada, the United States, and Europe.

What We Offer

  • Health & Wellness: Medical and dental coverage from Day 1, plus 24/7 telemedicine.
  • Financial Security: RRSP matching.
  • Work-Life Balance: Generous vacation and personal days.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Integrations
jobs.jd_full:
About Remote

Remote is solving modern organizations’ biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote.

With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role.

We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best-in-class HR platform.
If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work!

The Position

This is an exciting time to join Remote and make a difference in the global employment space as a Senior Product Manager, Customer Integrations In this role, you will sit at the unique intersection of Product Strategy and Partnerships.

Your mission is to build a thriving ecosystem of integrations and partnerships for Remote’s HR Platform. You won’t just manage the "how" of our integrations; you will also drive the "who" and the "why." You will be responsible for identifying high-impact partnership opportunities, pitching the Remote platform to external stakeholders, and building the technical bridges that allow our customers to manage recruitment, people and performance management without ever leaving their preferred tools.

By blending technical API expertise with a sharp business mind, you will directly influence Remote’s market expansion and long-term defensibility.

Key Responsibilities

  • Architect the Integration Strategy: Define a roadmap that balances technical scalability with commercial growth.
  • Strategic Business Development: Partner with GTM and Sales teams to identify, vet, and recruit high-value integration partners (eg. ATS, ERP, productivity, user provisioning, etc.).
  • Partner Evangelism & Pitching: Act as the technical face of Remote in partner discussions. You will clearly articulate the value proposition of integrating with Remote to external product and engineering leaders.
  • Design the "Win-Win" Integration: Work with partners to co-design integration flows that provide mutual value, ensuring that the final product drives high adoption for both parties.
  • Technical Discovery & Data Mapping: Lead deep-dive sessions into partner API capabilities (REST, Webhooks, OAuth) to ensure robust data synchronization and compliance across global jurisdictions.
  • Cross-Functional Orchestration: Act as the central "glue" between Engineering, Design, Sales, GTM, and CX to ensure every integration is technically sound and commercially successful.
  • Market Intelligence: Stay ahead of industry trends, identifying emerging players that could become key strategic partners for Remote.
  • Measure Ecosystem ROI: Establish and track KPIs not just for technical performance, but for business outcomes like partner-sourced leads, customer retention, and ecosystem revenue.

General Requirements

  • Senior-Level Experience: Track record of experience as a Product Manager with a strong focus on Integrations, APIs, or Platform ecosystems.
  • Proficiency in Cursor and/or Claude Code is required. You must be able to autonomously build, debug, and ship functional code/prototypes, not just write specs.
  • The "Product-Biz" Mindset: Proven ability to think commercially. You understand how an integration impacts the bottom line and can speak the language of both developers and business executives.
  • External Influence: Experience in a partner-facing role, with the ability to build rapport and negotiate technical/functional scope with external organizations.
  • Technical API Mastery: Deep familiarity with REST APIs, Webhooks, and authentication protocols. You can evaluate a partner's documentation and quickly determine if it meets Remote’s standards for security and scale.
  • Strong UX Sensibilities: A passion for creating "low-friction" experiences. You believe that even the most complex technical integration should feel simple to the end user.
  • Domain Expertise: Advantage if you have experience with global HR/Payroll tools (e.g., Workday, HiBob, Greenhouse, Netsuite).
  • Language: Business-level proficiency writing and speaking English.

Application process

  • Profile review (reviewed async)
  • Interview with recruiter
  • Interview with hiring manager
  • Product Deep Dive
  • Team Interview
  • Bar raiser interview
  • Interview with VP of Product
  • Background Check (Veremark)
  • Offer (shared async)

Remote's Total Rewards philosophy is to ensure fair, unbiased compensation and fair equity pay along with competitive benefits in all locations in which we operate. We do not agree to or encourage cheap-labor practices and therefore we ensure to pay above in-location rates. We hope to inspire other companies to support global talent-hiring and bring local wealth to developing countries.

At first glance our salary bands seem quite wide - here is some context. At Remote we have international operations and a globally distributed workforce. We use geo ranges to consider geographic pay differentials as part of our global compensation strategy to remain competitive in various markets while we hiring globally.

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location, and our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay for the successful candidate in this role is dependent upon many factors such as location, transferable or job-related skills, work experience, relevant training, business needs, and market demands. The base salary range may be subject to change.

At Remote, we foster internal mobility as a key element of our culture of employee growth and development, supported by a compensation philosophy that guarantees pay equity and fairness. Therefore, all compensation changes associated with an internal move will be reviewed by the Total Rewards & People Enablement team on a case by case basis.

The annual salary range for this full-time position is
$59,900—$134,800 USD

Benefits

Our full benefits & perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters:
  • work from anywhere
  • flexible paid time off
  • flexible working hours (we are async)
  • 16 weeks paid parental leave
  • mental health support services
  • stock options
  • learning budget
  • home office budget & IT equipment
  • budget for local in-person social events or co-working spaces

How you’ll plan your day (and life)

We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async.

You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your life-work balance is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs.

If that sounds like something you want, apply now!

How to apply

  1. Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format.
  2. We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote.
  3. If you don’t have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead.

Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask & encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter.

We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it’s important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to.

At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here.

Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.

jobs.title: Principal Product Manager - Platform

The industrial sector generates more data than almost any other, and Seeq is on a mission to help customers use it all to make better decisions. We're the market leader in advanced analytics for process manufacturing, helping the world's largest companies in pharma, energy, chemicals, and more turn massive streams of time-series data into real operational impact. We're approaching $100M ARR, recognized as a Leader by industry analysts, and are accelerating our AI-first product strategy to redefine what Decision Intelligence means for industry.

We're looking for a Principal Product Manager who thrives at the intersection of strategy and execution. Someone who can take an ambiguous problem space, apply rigorous thinking, and build outcome-based roadmaps that move our business forward. You'll own a significant product area within a platform used by some of the biggest enterprises on the planet, and you'll do it in a culture that values data over opinions, speed over perfection, and impact over activity.

This is not a feature factory. You will be expected to make hard tradeoffs, say no as much as you say yes, and ground every bet in evidence. If you get energy from designing for AI, using AI to accelerate your own ways of working, and operating with a high degree of autonomy, this role is for you.

Why you’re here

You have excellent communication skills, a problem-solving mindset, and a passion for the construction industry.

What you’re responsible for

jobs.title: Product Manager, Cards
jobs.jd_full:
About the Company

Rain makes the next generation of payments possible across the globe. We’re a lean and mighty team of passionate builders and veteran founders. Our infrastructure makes stablecoins usable in the real-world by powering card transactions, cross-border payments, B2B purchases, remittances, and more. We partner with fintechs, neobanks, and institutions to help them launch solutions that are global, inclusive, and efficient. You will have the opportunity to deliver massive impact at a hypergrowth company that is funded by some of the top investors in fintech, crypto, and SaaS, including Sapphire Ventures, Norwest, Galaxy Ventures, Lightspeed, Khosla, and several more. If you’re curious, bold, and excited to help shape a borderless financial future, we’d love to talk.

Our Ethos

We believe in an open and flat structure. You will be able to grow into the role that most aligns with your goals. Our team members at all levels have the freedom to explore ideas and impact the roadmap and vision of our company.

About the Role

Rain is hiring a Senior Product Manager to own cards — one of the most strategically important surfaces at the company. Cards are how stablecoins meet real-world spend, and this role will shape the product, platform, and growth decisions that define that experience for our partners and their end users.

This is a senior individual contributor role. You’ll operate as the directly responsible PM for cards end to end: the roadmap, the cardholder experience, the platform capabilities that power it, and the activation and growth loops that make the product stick. You’ll work closely with engineering, design, partnerships, and directly with our fintech, neobank, and institutional customers.

What You’ll Do

• Own the roadmap for Rain’s cards product — cardholder experience, platform capabilities, and growth — and drive it end to end

• Partner deeply with engineering on platform and infrastructure tradeoffs (issuing flows, authorization behavior, ledger design, network integrations) and translate them into partner-facing product decisions

• Define and ship the card experience our fintech, neobank, and institutional partners put in front of their users — onboarding, activation, spend controls, and lifecycle

• Build and tune the activation and growth levers that drive adoption, usage, and retention of cards across partner programs

• Translate complex payments and stablecoin infrastructure into intuitive product surfaces

• Gather feedback directly from partners, distill it into sharp prioritization, and push the team toward the highest-leverage work

• Measure ruthlessly, iterate quickly, and set a bar others want to work to

What We’re Looking For

• 8-10 years of product management experience, with a track record of shipping products from zero-to-one through scale at a product-led company with a strong engineering culture and high bar for quality

• Exceptional product judgment and analytical rigor — you’re someone others describe as one of the sharpest PMs they’ve worked with, and you consistently raise the bar for the people around you

• A senior, autonomous operator who can define the problem, drive the plan, and own the outcome without needing close direction

• Strong technical fluency (APIs, payments systems, blockchain concepts) and the instinct to pull on technical threads yourself rather than defer to engineering

• Genuinely excited to live in the details — you believe product quality is won in the specifics and are energized by the work to get them right

• Excellent written and verbal communication; you can operate with sophisticated external partners and internal engineering teams with equal ease

• Proven ability to ship quickly, measure adoption, and iterate in a fast-moving environment

Nice to Have, But Not Mandatory

• Experience with card issuing, payments, or cross-border fintech products

• Exposure to stablecoins, crypto, or smart contract ecosystems

• Previous startup or hypergrowth environment experience

Things that enable a fulfilling, healthy, and happy experience at Rain:

• Unlimited time off 🌴 Unlimited vacation can be daunting, so we require Rainmakers to take at least 10 days off.

• Flexible working ☕ We support a flexible workplace. If you feel comfortable at home, please work from home. If you’d like to work with others in an office, feel free to come in. We want everyone to be able to work in the environment in which they are their most confident and productive selves. New Rainmakers will receive a stipend to create a comfortable home environment.

• Easy to access benefits 🧠 For US Rainmakers, we offer comprehensive health, dental, and vision plans for you and your dependents, as well as a 100% company subsidized life insurance plan.

• Retirement goals 💡 Plan for the future with confidence. We offer a 401(k) with a 4% company match.

• Equity plan 📦 We offer every Rainmaker an equity option plan so we can all benefit from our success.

• Rain Cards 🌧️ We want Rainmakers to be knowledgeable about our core products and services. To support this mission, we issue a card for our team to use for testing.

• Health and Wellness 📚 High performance begins from within. Rainmakers are welcome to use their card for eligible health and wellness spending like gym memberships/fitness classes, massages, acupuncture — whatever recharges you!

• Team summits ✨ Summits play an important role at Rain! Time spent together helps us get to know each other, strengthen our relationships, and build a common destiny. Expect team and company off-sites both domestically and internationally.

jobs.title: IAM Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

About AbbVie

AbbVie’s mission is to discover and deliver innovative medicines and solutions that solve serious health issues today and address the medical challenges of tomorrow. We strive to have a remarkable impact on people’s lives across several key therapeutic areas including immunology, oncology and neuroscience - and products and services in our Allergan Aesthetics portfolio. For more information about AbbVie, please visit us at www.abbvie.com. Follow @abbvie on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X and YouTube.

Job Description

This position is part of AbbVie’s Information Security & Risk Management (ISRM) team within Business Technology Solutions. The Identity & Access Management (IAM) team is responsible for the development, implementation, and oversight of the organization’s IAM strategy, ensuring secure, efficient, and compliant access to systems and data. The IAM team supports all verticals including User Lifecycle Management, Privileged Account Management, Directory Services, MFA/Federation/SSO, Access Certifications, Role Based Access Control, External Identity Management, and Application & System Integration. We are here to put our partners in a position to succeed by providing the knowledge, tools, and support they need to effectively use data and technology while managing risk.

This position can be virtual anywhere in the U.S.

Take the next step in your career with an organization that strategically invests in the long-term health of the company, its technology and its people. The AbbVie Business Technology Solutions (BTS) team shapes the digital transformation necessary for our treatments to positively impact patients’ lives. In the role of Program Manager, Identity & Access Management, you’ll tackle complex challenges as you develop and diversify your professional experiences while helping others grow and develop their skills.

In this role, you’ll be responsible for:

  • Leading the strategy, execution, and governance of multi-year IAM transformation initiatives across the enterprise
  • Defining program objectives, success criteria, and roadmaps to deliver technology capabilities and process enhancements for IAM
  • Managing large, cross-functional project teams—including technical, business, and vendor resources—across multiple workstreams
  • Overseeing the implementation of IAM solutions, including onboarding of applications, privileged access management (PAM), identity lifecycle management, and access governance
  • Driving change management efforts to achieve broad business adoption and process optimization, including stakeholder engagement, training, and communications
  • Collaborating with IT, Security, Compliance, HR, and business units to ensure seamless integration and alignment with other enterprise programs
  • Identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks and issues affecting program delivery, scope, and adoption
  • Managing program budgets, financials, vendor contracts, and resource allocations
  • Maintaining high standards in documentation, reporting, and status communication to executive leadership and stakeholders
  • Supporting audit, regulatory compliance (e.g., SOX, GDPR, PCI-DSS), and remediation activities related to IAM
  • Tracking key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to measure program success and drive continuous improvement
  • Developing and enhancing governance frameworks, policies, and process controls for IAM

Qualifications

jobs.title: Program Manager Enterprise Business Systems
jobs.jd_full:
About Acquia

Acquia empowers the world’s most ambitious brands to create digital customer experiences that matter. With open source Drupal at its core, the Acquia Digital Experience Platform (DXP) enables marketers, developers, and IT operations teams at thousands of global organizations to rapidly compose and deploy digital products and services that engage customers, enhance conversions, and help businesses stand out.

Headquartered in the U.S., Acquia has been named a top software company by The Software Report, rated a leader by the analyst community, and named a top place to work by the Boston Globe and the Boston Business Journal. We are Acquia. We are building for the future and we want you to be a part of it!

Position Overview

The Acquia Enterprise Integrations Team, one of the teams in the Enterprise Business Systems organization, builds, maintains, and improves internal system integrations and tools. The  software engineers on the team primarily build applications that connect the systems that help make Acquia run.

We are looking to add a Program Manager to our team to help guide our ship and to serve as the central point of contact for our applications. In this role you will help define our roadmap and direct us down the right path. You will be responsible for gathering requirements from stakeholders, identifying risks, determining what will be actioned and what will not be actioned, prioritizing and scheduling work, coordinating activities, communicating to stakeholders and team members, and managing projects to on-time completion. You will report directly to the Senior Director of Enterprise Business Systems and work closely with the Enterprise Integrations team of engineers that are distributed globally.

Responsibilities

  • Meeting with key internal stakeholders, gathering requirements, prioritizing work and  determining the roadmap of the Enterprise Integrations Team
  • Ensuring that project requests align with organizational goals and objectives
  • Driving projects to on-time completion
  • Communicating with management, stakeholders and the engineering team
  • Managing the product backlog
  • Producing Scrum artifacts
  • Scheduling and leading stakeholder meetings, daily standups, sprint planning sessions, sprint demonstrations, and sprint retrospectives

Must Have Skills & Traits

  • Strong project management skills
  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate and work in a team environment
  • Good communication skills, both verbal (English) and written
  • Ability to foresee potential problems and to offer solutions
  • Excellent organizational skills and the ability to multitask
  • Good negotiation skills and the ability to say “no” in a constructive fashion
  • Results oriented, drive to succeed
  • Original thinker, is willing to try new things
  • Proactive, does not wait to be told what to do, seeks out opportunities
  • Ability to hit the ground running and adjust quickly
  • Extremely collaborative and willing to assist teammates on any issue
  • Working knowledge of Agile methodology

Nice To Have

  • Experience with Atlassian Tool Set (Confluence, Jira)
  • Experience with Google Tool Set (Docs, Sheets, Slides)
  • Comfortable using Artificial Intelligence (AI) Tools

This job is not

  • You will not be meeting with external customers or clients
  • You will not have any direct reports (will not be managing people)

We are an organization that embraces innovation and the potential of AI to enhance our processes and improve our work. We are always looking for individuals who are open to learning new technologies and collaborating with AI tools to achieve our goals.

Acquia is proud to provide best-in-class benefits to help our employees and their families maintain a healthy body and mind. Core Benefits include: competitive healthcare coverage, wellness programs, take it when you need it time off, parental leave, recognition programs, and much more!

Individuals seeking employment at Acquia are considered without regard to race, color, religion, caste, creed, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation. Whatever you answer will not be considered in the hiring process or thereafter.

jobs.title: Marketing Program Manager Wealth Management
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Blend is a premier AI services provider, committed to co-creating meaningful impact for its clients through the power of data science, AI, technology, and people. With a mission to fuel bold visions, Blend tackles significant challenges by seamlessly aligning human expertise with artificial intelligence. The company is dedicated to unlocking value and fostering innovation for its clients by harnessing world-class people and data-driven strategy. We believe that the power of people and AI can have a meaningful impact on your world, creating more fulfilling work and projects for our people and clients.

Job Description

Our client, a global financial services institution, is seeking a Marketing Program Manager to support a high-performing Wealth Management marketing organization.  This team is responsible for delivering integrated marketing initiatives across a portfolio of client segments and financial products.

This role is ideal for a senior-level program manager who thrives in complex, highly regulated environments and brings a strong combination of marketing domain knowledge, governance expertise, and cross-functional leadership.  You will play a critical role in driving execution, establishing structure, and ensuring alignment across multiple concurrent marketing initiatives.

The Details:

  • Location: Remote within the U.S.
  • Duration: 12-month consulting role with potential for extension (must be able to work on Blend’s W2 without requiring employer sponsorship either now or in the future)
  • Benefits: We do offer benefits to our full-time consultants, including Health, Vision, Dental, 401K plan, Life Insurance, Employee Assistance Programs, and an incredibly supportive team cheering you on!

What you’ll do:

The Program Manager will serve as a central driver of execution and governance across a portfolio of strategic marketing initiatives.  This role will partner closely with marketing leaders, strategy teams, and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure programs are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned to business objectives.

This is a highly visible role requiring strong organizational discipline, stakeholder management, and the ability to build and enforce governance frameworks in a matrixed environment.

Program Governance & Structure

  • Establish and maintain program governance frameworks, including project charters, RACI matrices, decision logs, and escalation protocols.
  • Ensure consistent oversight and alignment across multiple marketing workstreams.
  • Drive accountability across stakeholders without direct authority.

Roadmap & Execution Management

  • Develop and manage integrated marketing roadmaps, including timelines, milestones, dependencies, and deliverables.
  • Track progress against strategic priorities to ensure on-time, on-budget delivery.
  • Identify risks, issues, and blockers, and proactively implement mitigation strategies.

Cross-Functional Coordination

  • Partner with marketing managers, strategy leads, and execution teams to ensure seamless delivery across initiatives.
  • Coordinate workflows across business, creative, legal, compliance, and operational teams.
  • Manage approval processes and ensure all deliverables meet required standards and checkpoints.

Program Operations & Administration

  • Lead day-to-day program management activities, including scheduling and facilitating meetings, capturing action items, and tracking follow-ups.
  • Maintain detailed documentation to ensure transparency, accountability, and audit readiness.

Stakeholder Communication & Reporting

  • Deliver regular status updates and executive-level reporting, including progress summaries, risks, and key decisions.
  • Communicate effectively across all levels of the organization, including senior leadership.
  • Ensure alignment across stakeholders in a highly matrixed environment.

Qualifications

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred.
  • 8+ years of experience in program or project management within complex, enterprise environments.
  • Proven experience managing multiple large-scale, cross-functional initiatives simultaneously.
  • Prior experience supporting marketing organizations or campaign delivery teams.
  • Background in financial services or wealth management

Technical & Functional Skills

  • Deep expertise in program governance, process design, and execution management.
  • Strong understanding of marketing campaign lifecycles, approval workflows, and go-to-market execution.
  • Experience managing legal and compliance approval processes within regulated environments.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint) for reporting and executive communication.

Soft Skills

  • Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to influence and drive alignment across teams without direct authority.
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail and follow-through.
  • Proactive, solutions-oriented mindset with strong problem-solving capabilities.
  • Comfortable operating in fast-paced, highly matrixed environments with competing priorities.

Additional Information

The starting pay range for this role is $60.00 - $65.00 per hour. Actual compensation within the range will be dependent on several factors including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, certifications, training, and location. It is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range and determining factors for compensation are considered for each individual circumstance. BLEND360 also offers a competitive benefits program to meet the health and financial well-being of our team and their families. You can look forward to a range of benefits including medical, dental, vision, 401K, PTO, commuter benefits, spending accounts, life insurance, disability coverage, and EAPs.

A diverse workforce is a strong workforce

To deliver growth at BLEND360 and for our clients, we believe as a Talent Solutions Company, we have a responsibility and unique opportunity to positively impact the workforce. Diversity has played a critical role in our history, our growth, and continues to have a profound impact on our success.  We are determined to have equality in the workplace, within our team and as an extension of our clients’ team.

BLEND360 is an equal opportunity employer.

Eight ProSidian Global Competencies

  • Business Tools – understanding and proficiency with business tools and technology, including Microsoft Office.  The ideal candidate is advanced with Excel, Access, Outlook, PowerPoint, and Word; proficient with Adobe Acrobat, data analytics tools, and Visio; and able to quickly learn other tools as needed.

  • Competitive Compensation: The pay range is competitive and includes health benefits, pre-tax employee benefits, and incentives. The company contributes a fixed dollar amount each month to the selected medical and dental plans, with the contributions deducted pre-tax.
  • Group Medical / Dental / Vision Health Insurance Benefits: ProSidian partners with providers to offer eligible employees a range of medical and dental plans, including high-deductible health plans and PPOs, as well as plans for high and low vision.
  • 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan: The 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan lets eligible employees save for retirement with various investment options and support from a financial planner. It operates as a pre-tax Safe Harbor plan with a company match.
  • Vacation and Paid Time-Off (PTO) Benefits: Eligible employees can use PTO for vacations, doctor’s appointments, or personal events.  These benefits include 2 weeks of vacation, three sick days, 10 ProSidian holidays, and government holidays.
  • Pre-Tax Payment Programs: Pre-Tax Payment Programs are premium-only plans (POPs) that provide eligible employees with a comprehensive Flexible Spending Account (FSA) Plan and associated tax benefits.
  • Purchasing Discounts & Savings Plans: We aim for your financial success with a Purchasing Discounts and Savings Plan via the Corporate Perks Benefit Program, offering eligible employees discounts on everyday products and services.
  • Security Clearance: Due to the nature of our consulting engagements, a security clearance is necessary for engagement teams managing sensitive work in the Federal Marketplace.  Security clearance is a valuable asset in your professional portfolio, enhancing your credentials.
  • ProSidian Employee & Contractor Referral Bonus Program: ProSidian can offer up to $5,000 for referrals who stay employed for 90 days, based on candidates submitted through our Referral Program.
  • Performance Incentives: Due to the nature of our consulting work, performance incentives are tied to each new client that employees assist in pursuing and supporting.
  • Flexible Spending Account: FSAs let you pre-tax cover eligible healthcare and daycare costs. You estimate your annual expenses and allocate part of each paycheck to your FSA.
  • Supplemental Life/Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance: If you want extra protection for yourself and your eligible dependents, you can choose supplemental life insurance.  D&D covers death or dismemberment resulting solely from an accident.
  • Short- and Long-Term Disability Insurance: Disability insurance plans are designed to offer income protection during your recovery from a disability.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Integration Platform
jobs.jd_full:
ezCater is the #1 food tech platform for workplaces in the US. The company makes it easy for any organization to manage its food needs and order from over 125,000 restaurants nationwide. For workplaces, ezCater provides flexible and scalable solutions for everything from employee meal programs to one-off meetings, all backed by beyond helpful 24⁄7 service and business-grade reliability. For restaurant partners, ezCater helps grow their business by bringing them new high-value customers and large orders.

Integration is ezCater’s competitive moat. The speed at which we can connect with corporate customers’ procurement, expense, and HR systems—and with restaurant POS platforms, delivery partners, and other technology providers—directly determines how fast we win and retain the highest-value accounts in our market. Today, our integrations are built by separate teams using different patterns, tooling, and standards. That approach got us here, but it won’t get us where we need to go. We need to move dramatically faster.

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to define, build, and own a unified Integration Platform as part of our next-generation platform, Nova. Your mission: hyper-accelerate ezCater’s ability to build, ship, and scale integrations across every partner type. You will create the shared services, reusable connectors, and developer tooling that turn what is currently a months-long, bespoke engineering effort into a repeatable, configurable, fast deployment. Critically, you’ll also own the orchestration layer that binds individual integrations into end-to-end business processes—ensuring that a corporate customer onboarding, for example, seamlessly coordinates HRIS roster sync, policy assignment, SSO provisioning, and procurement setup as a single, observable workflow rather than a collection of disconnected handoffs. This role sits within the Platform domain and reports to the VP of Product Management for Platforms. You’ll work across the full spectrum of ezCater’s integration surface—procurement systems like Coupa and Ariba, expense platforms like Concur and Ramp, HRIS/SCIM roster sync, POS platforms like Toast and Olo, delivery dispatch APIs, and more—building the foundation that lets every product team integrate and orchestrate faster than any competitor in our space.

What You’ll Do:

  • Define the vision, strategy, and roadmap for ezCater’s Integration Platform with one overriding objective: dramatically compress the time it takes to build, launch, and scale integrations with any third party.
  • Design and deliver core platform capabilities—reusable connector framework, integration modeling, event-driven messaging, business-process orchestration, failure handling, reconciliation, credential management, and observability—so that product teams can stand up new integrations in days or weeks instead of months.
  • Prioritize the corporate-customer integration surface (procurement, expense management, HRIS/SCIM, SSO, analytics delivery) as the highest-impact acceleration target, directly enabling faster enterprise sales cycles and onboarding.
  • Define and build the business-process orchestration layer: a platform capability that composes individual integrations into cross-domain workflows (e.g., corporate customer onboarding that coordinates roster sync, policy setup, procurement connection, and SSO provisioning as a single managed process with visibility into state, progress, and failures at every step).
  • Create a commercial-grade developer experience—integration catalog, developer documentation, self-service onboarding, sandbox environments, and API reference—that lets both internal teams and external partners integrate with minimal hand-holding.
  • Build an operational UI for integration health monitoring, issue tracking, alerting, and escalation, giving operations teams real-time visibility and reducing mean-time-to-resolution across all active integrations.
  • Establish a library of configurable, composable connectors and data-mapping templates that eliminate repetitive engineering work and turn each new integration into a configuration exercise rather than a development project.
  • Partner closely with the corporate-customer, supplier, and delivery product teams to understand their integration backlogs and ensure the platform directly unblocks their highest-priority partner requests.
  • Drive the migration of existing integrations from ezRails onto Nova, prioritizing based on business impact and using each migration as an opportunity to prove and refine the platform’s acceleration capabilities.
  • Own and obsess over time-to-integrate as the platform’s north-star metric, alongside integration reliability, platform adoption, and partner satisfaction.
  • Tell a compelling story using research and data to evangelize the roadmap internally and with key partners, making the case that integration velocity is ezCater’s competitive edge.
  • Travel to Boston quarterly for planning and business reviews.
  • Have fun building impactful platform infrastructure with awesome people.

What You Have: (insert details here in bullet format)

  • 5+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience in platform, infrastructure, or developer-tools products.
  • Track record building integration platforms, API platforms, iPaaS, or middleware products—you understand connectors, event-driven architecture, and data mapping at a deep level.
  • Experience designing and implementing platform services that are consumed by multiple internal product teams (platform-as-a-product mindset).
  • Strong understanding of enterprise integration patterns: REST/GraphQL APIs, webhooks, message queues, ETL/ELT, OAuth/SAML, and data reconciliation. Experience with workflow orchestration engines or business-process automation (e.g., step functions, saga patterns, BPMN) is highly valued.
  • Deep analytic ability, comfort working with and manipulating data, building hypotheses to inform innovative solutions.
  • Experience working in marketplace or multi-sided-platform businesses is a strong plus.
  • Familiarity with enterprise software ecosystems (procurement, expense management, HRIS, POS) is a strong plus.
  • Strong teamwork and collaboration skills; you’re happy spending much of your day collaborating with team members in different functional areas.
  • Experience with migration programs (monolith-to-microservices, platform re-architecture).
  • Background in B2B or enterprise SaaS integration strategy.
  • Experience building or managing developer portals and external API programs.
  • Ability to travel up to 5 days per quarter for Together Weeks, team gatherings and other events, when applicable.

The national total targetcash compensation range for this position, including base salary and bonus target, is $161,000–$213,000 annually.*

*Please note: Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors, including prior experience, expertise and region & may vary from the amount above. This range does not represent additional compensation benefits (such as equity, 401K or medical, dental or vision insurance).

ezCater does not sponsor applicants for work visas or legal permanent residence.

What You’ll Get from Us:

You’ll get a terrifically compelling experience in an innovative, high performing environment. You’ll get to work with engaged and passionate colleagues on challenging and impactful projects. You will have opportunities to grow in your career, and work in a  place that values work/life harmony.

Oh, and you’ll get all this: Market competitive salary, stock options that you’ll help make worth a lot, 12 paid holidays, flexible PTO, 401K with ezCater match, health/dental/FSA, long-term disability insurance, mental health and family planning resources, remote-hybrid work from our awesome Boston office OR your home OR a mixture of both home and office, a tremendous amount of responsibility and autonomy, wicked awesome co-workers, employee meal program (and many more goodies) when you’re in our office, and knowing that you helped transform the food for work space.

ezCater is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace humans of every background, appearance, race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, and disability status. At the same time, we do not employ jerks, even brilliant ones. Following a conditional offer of employment, ezCater may require a background check.

For information on how ezCater collects and uses job applicants’ personal information, please visit our Job Applicant Privacy Policy.
